LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ROBERT COPELAND DEMURRER TO DEFENDANTS' AMENDED ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 1ST AND 2ND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 5TH DEFENSE (ADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW) WHICH IS NOT A DEFENSE, BUT A FACTOR CONSIDERED BY THE COURT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO REMAINING DEFENSES. WAIVER AND ESTOPPEL ARE SEPARATE DEFENSES, UNCLEAN HANDS AND LACHES ARE SEPARATE DEFENSES, ALL MUST BE AFFIRMED SEPARATELY. DEFENSES MUST BE PLEAD WITH PARTICULARITY AND ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers Toi Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar May-06-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Tim Travelstead, Atty. for Defendant and opposing party: Spruce Restaurant
No appearances made by Plantiff/moving party.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFF ROBERT COPELAND'S Motion To Compel Further Answers To Special Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant is ordered to respond to the interrogatories as they seek names of all employees and their contact information. However, all of the remaining information shall be provided in a format which removes all names and other identifying information. Defendant shall create an index correlating the names of the employees to the random number assigned to each employee. That index shall be maintained by Defendants until the class is certified.
MODIFICATION:
- Respond within 20 days of notice of the order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Defts' Amended Answer To Pltf'S First Amended Complaint;
Off Calendar Apr-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ROBERT COPELAND Demurrer to Defendants' Amended Answer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to 1st and 2nd affirmative defenses. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 5th defense (adequate remedy at law) which is not a defense, but a factor considered by the court. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to remaining defenses. Waiver and estoppel are separate defenses, unclean hands and laches are separate defenses, all must be affirmed separately. Defenses must be plead with particularity and allege facts in support. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
